<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Ground shadow -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="445" rx="150" ry="22" fill="#000000" opacity="0.08"/>

  <!-- Generator body -->
  <rect x="150" y="165" width="212" height="240" rx="22" fill="#2b2f36" stroke="#111" stroke-width="4"/>
  <rect x="178" y="195" width="156" height="56" rx="12" fill="#3b4048" stroke="#111" stroke-width="3"/>

  <!-- Control panel circles -->
  <circle cx="206" cy="223" r="10" fill="#d9d9d9"/>
  <circle cx="256" cy="223" r="10" fill="#d9d9d9"/>
  <circle cx="306" cy="223" r="10" fill="#d9d9d9"/>

  <!-- Side vent lines -->
  <g stroke="#1a1d23" stroke-width="3" opacity="0.55">
    <line x1="168" y1="265" x2="168" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="187" y1="265" x2="187" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="206" y1="265" x2="206" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="225" y1="265" x2="225" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="244" y1="265" x2="244" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="263" y1="265" x2="263" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="282" y1="265" x2="282" y2="320"/>
    <line x1="301" y1="265" x2="301" y2="320"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Handle -->
  <path d="M205 165
           C205 125, 307 125, 307 165" fill="none" stroke="#1a1d23" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M228 165
           C228 140, 284 140, 284 165" fill="none" stroke="#2b2f36" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Wheels / feet -->
  <g fill="#2b2f36" stroke="#111" stroke-width="4">
    <rect x="170" y="388" width="62" height="28" rx="10"/>
    <rect x="280" y="388" width="62" height="28" rx="10"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Thunder / power symbol -->
  <g transform="translate(0,0)">
    <path d="M255 300
             L230 350
             L258 350
             L240 398
             L295 330
             L268 330
             Z" fill="#ffd54a" stroke="#a46a00" stroke-width="3"/>
    <path d="M255 300
             L230 350
             L258 350
             L240 398
             L295 330
             L268 330
             Z" fill="#ffd54a" opacity="0.25" transform="translate(3,6)"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Exhaust / outlet -->
  <rect x="225" y="140" width="62" height="40" rx="14" fill="#3b4048" stroke="#111" stroke-width="3"/>
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="156" rx="26" ry="12" fill="#1a1d23" opacity="0.35"/>

  <!-- Cable to the right -->
  <path d="M362 275
           C410 280, 420 315, 452 332" fill="none" stroke="#1a1d23" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <path d="M450 332
           C468 342, 468 365, 450 376" fill="none" stroke="#1a1d23" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  <rect x="441" y="372" width="28" height="18" rx="6" fill="#2b2f36" stroke="#111" stroke-width="3"/>

  <!-- Small spark near cable -->
  <path d="M420 315
           L412 335
           L426 335
           L420 355
           L438 330
           L424 330
           Z" fill="#7cffc8" stroke="#149a54" stroke-width="2"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

message.reasoning:
**Identifying electrical items**

I’m looking at an image of something like a suitcase or briefcase with a lightning bolt and a cable, which suggests it could be related to a portable battery or charger. I think it indicates a "powerbank," but "battery" could also work. The lightning symbol and the cable point towards electrical connections. I'll list ideas related to this in order: charger, battery, powerbank, power, electricity, voltage, and more, ensuring they're one word and unique.
